title: Chilled Fruit Soup
ingredients: 6 medium-sized ripe peaches, peeled, pitted and sliced
¼ cup dry white wine (non-alcoholic works fine)
1 medium-size ripe cantaloupe, peeled, seeded and cut into ½-inch pieces
Garnish: fresh mint and fresh blueberries

In heavy saucepan, combine peaches, wine, lemon juice, honey, cinnamon and nutmeg. Heat to boiling. Lower heat, cover and simmer for 10 minutes. Remove from heat, cool to room temperature, and puree in blender or food processor.

In blender or food processor, puree orange juice and ¾ cantaloupe until smooth. In large bowl, combine peach and cantaloupe mixtures. Chop remaining cantaloupe and stir into soup. Cover and chill thoroughly. Serve soup in chilled bowls or container. Garnish with fresh mint and blueberries. This tastes like summer.
This makes 4 servings – I also double or triple the recipe. Really pretty to serve in small decorative glasses at a tea or shower party.
notes: This recipe always reminds me of Deb Larsen. She loved this recipe. I wish I had made it for her more.
